header_summary:
The release of Bitcoin Core 29.3 introduces significant enhancements, including bug fixes, performance improvements, and wallet functionality updates, aimed at bolstering the network's security and user experience. This version, tested across major operating systems, emphasizes improvements in peer-to-peer network interactions and mining efficiency, alongside documentation updates to aid developers and users alike. The development team encourages community feedback through the GitHub issue tracker to continually refine the software ([source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAO3Wf41sAHeZ-KR1mp=xNuydstcMk9R93ou5Jobmq9ngn7TKXA@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m110222ead81e7ac2f1becbfe3e4f49db3200dafc)).

In parallel, discussions surrounding BIP392 for Silent Payment Output Script Descriptors and the Hourglass proposal v2 reflect the Bitcoin community's proactive approach in addressing fund recovery efficiency and security vulnerabilities, respectively. BIP392 suggests adding annotations to descriptors to facilitate fund recovery without compromising efficiency, while the Hourglass proposal v2 aims to limit the potential market impact of mass liquidations by restricting Bitcoin spends from P2PK outputs ([BIP proposal details](https://github.com/craigraw/bips/blob/descriptorannotations/bip-descriptorannotations.mediawiki), [Hourglass Proposal V2](https://github.com/cryptoquick/bips/blob/hourglass-v2/bip-hourglass-v2.mediawiki)).

Moreover, the integration of SHRINCS with CAT through covenants showcases innovative approaches to enhancing privacy and security in cryptocurrency transactions, balancing cost and security considerations. Additionally, an analysis of OP_RETURN output usage between September 2025 and February 2026 reveals insights into blockchain transaction trends, highlighting the variance in OP_RETURN data sizes and the strategic burning of satoshis to manage transaction space efficiently ([SHRINCS and CAT integration](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/shrincs-324-byte-stateful-post-quantum-signatures-with-static-backups/2158/16), [OP_RETURN output statistics](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/recent-op-return-output-statistics/2248)).

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v29.3 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core 29.3, featuring bug fixes and improvements, is available for download.
- It introduces updates to P2P networking and wallet functionality, plus mining and build system fixes.
- Supported on Linux Kernel 3.17+, macOS 13+, and Windows 10+, with comprehensive changes detailed.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Output Script Descriptor Annotations
recent_posts.summary:
- BIP392's discussion highlights recovery issues due to intensive scanning needs.
- The proposal suggests adding metadata annotations to descriptors for efficient fund recovery.
- Annotations include keys like starting block height and gap limit, essential for fund recovery.

recent_posts.title: Hourglass V2 Update
recent_posts.summary:
- The Hourglass proposal revision limits Bitcoin spending from P2PK outputs to one per block.
- Details of the revised proposal are documented and available on GitHub for further exploration.
- Community feedback is encouraged to refine the proposal, highlighting the importance of collaborative efforts.

recent_posts.title: SHRINCS: 324-byte stateful post-quantum signatures with static backups
recent_posts.summary:
- Implementing SHRINCS with CAT enhances CoinSwap transactions by improving security and efficiency.
- Cost and computational challenges are offset by the privacy and security benefits for users.
- This approach showcases advanced uses of blockchain for secure and private financial transactions.

recent_posts.title: Recent OP_RETURN output statistics
recent_posts.summary:
- Analysis between blocks 915800 and 936000 shows 24,362,310 transactions with OP_RETURN outputs.
- Transactions over this period had 473,815,552 bytes of OP_RETURN data, highlighting varied usage patterns.
- A total of 1,463,488 satoshis were burned in 34,283 transactions through OP_RETURN outputs.

recent_posts.title: Hourglass V2 Update
recent_posts.summary:
- Hourglass addresses P2PK coin movement issues by incentivizing early transfers before a set block height.
- It aims to differentiate genuine holders from quantum attackers, offering a chance to reclaim assets post-activation.
- The proposal anticipates reduced P2PK transactions post-activation, encouraging late movers and protecting against quantum threats.

active_posts.title: Addressing remaining points on BIP 54
active_posts.summary:
- BIP 54 debate resurfaces with critiques on coinbase transaction nLockTime optimization loss.
- Jeremy Rubin suggests a new sparse Merkle tree to address 64-byte transaction limits.
- Consensus emphasizes pragmatic solutions over major protocol changes for Bitcoin's stability.

active_posts.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances Bitcoin's security against quantum computing and algorithm breaks.
- It introduces a dual signature system with a primary and a secondary algorithm for emergencies.
- The system includes infrastructure updates and uses BIP 360 for future cryptographic threats.

active_posts.title: Bip54: add sequence, timestamp (and version) to GBT
active_posts.summary:
- BIP54 aims to standardize `nLockTime` and `nSequence` in the coinbase transaction to aid future compatibility.
- It introduces `coinbase_locktime`, `coinbase_sequence`, and `coinbase_version` keys for better transaction guidelines.
- Compliance with these changes ensures mining software is prepared for potential protocol adjustments without further updates.

active_posts.title: Gossip Observer: New project to monitor the Lightning P2P network
active_posts.summary:
- The project monitors the Lightning gossip network, analyzing message dynamics since PlebFi Miami.
- Findings show a decrease in message convergence delay and potential network inefficiencies.
- Future goals include expanding infrastructure for broader data collection and community engagement.

active_posts.title: Major BIP 360 Update
active_posts.summary:
- Ethan Heilman and team rewrote BIP 360 for clarity and quantum risk mitigation.
- The rewrite introduces "Pay-to-Tapscript-Hash (P2TSH)" to enhance ECC's quantum resistance.
- New test vectors in Python and Rust are included, inviting community review and collaboration.

active_posts.title: Benchmarking Bitcoin Script Evaluation for the Varops Budget (Great Script Restoration)
active_posts.summary:
- A new Tapscript leaf version is proposed to mitigate denial-of-service attacks in Bitcoin.
- The solution introduces a varops budget linked to transaction weight to manage compute units.
- The community is encouraged to test the proposal via benchmarks and contribute feedback.

today_in_history_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Simple Proof-of-Reserves Transactions
today_in_history_posts.summary: - A proposed proof format aims to standardize combining proofs and metadata using Protocol Buffers.  - Concerns were raised about its adoption due to controversy related to BIP70 content.  - Suggestions included adding a Merkle proof and addressing invalid transactions consuming node memory.

today_in_history_posts.title: Payee pay fee
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Cezary Dziemian inquired about a payment solution where the payee covers the fee.
- He highlighted a problem with withdrawals via LN due to unawareness of fees.
- Suggests users should withdraw total funds and have fees deducted afterward.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin scalability targets a billion weekly users through 50,000 off-chain "bitcoin banks."- Off-chain solutions include federated sidechains, Chaumian ecash, and coinpools without third-party reliance.
- Emphasizes on non-centralization and efficient Layer 2 protocols, with a focus on security and regulatory compliance.
